feed.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms

entry.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
entry.summary: In a recent discussion on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List, Jonas Nick initiated a conversation that was later continued by Ethan Heilman, who emphasized the significance of deploying GSR or a similar mechanism within BIP360. This approach, as discussed, allows for the creation and evolution of DSA2 without necessitating additional forks, an aspect highlighted as potentially beneficial due to the assumption that most of these addresses might never be utilized for spending. The dialogue further explores the viewpoint presented by Sipa during an Optech podcast, where the inappropriateness of this method for DSA1/3 is outlined. Specifically, it's underscored that there exists a stringent requirement for a thoroughly vetted ecosystem in scenarios involving regular spends, lightning transactions, and aggregation processes. This exchange underscores the complexities and considerations involved in evolving digital signature algorithms within the Bitcoin network, reflecting on both technical and practical implications of such advancements.
